news.title: Four Observations from the World Cup third place match
news.lede: The third place match of this World Cup was something else. Four goals from England in the first half made it seem that we were not going to see a close game, but France had something to say about it. We saw records matched and overcome, and two key goals in the Golden Boot race. This was the highest scoring match in this edition of the tournament — surpassing Germany’s 7-1 win over Curacao.

news.title: Football vs. fútbol: Nine reasons I'll always take the NFL over World Cup | Opinion
news.lede: Eye of the beholder, etc., etc. For the fútbol faithful, the World Cup excitement is building to a crescendo ahead of Sunday's final between defending champion Argentina and Spain. But for the football faithful? We've been subjected to plenty.

news.title: World Cup prize money: England’s third-place payout and the full list of team earnings
news.lede: England is heading home from the 2026 FIFA World Cup with the bronze medal — and an extra $2 million. England's wild 6-4 victory over France in Saturday's third-place match secured a $29 million prize, while fourth-place France will receive $27 million. As FIFA announced in December, the largest World Cup ever also features the largest prize pool in tournament history. A total of $655 million will be shared among the 48 teams, with payouts ranging from $9 million to $50 million depending on how far each nation advanced.

news.title: Harry Kane doesn't play in wild World Cup third-place match vs. France
news.lede: MIAMI GARDENS — England star Harry Kane did not play in the World Cup third-place match against France on Saturday, July 18 at Hard Rock Stadium. Kane was on the bench throughout South Florida's final match of the World Cup. He watched Bukayo Saka, Declan Rice and Ezri Konsa score goals to give England a 4-0 halftime lead, only for France to claw its way back into the match with four second-half goals. Saka converted a penalty in the 87th minute to secure a hat trick and give England a 5-3 lead.

news.title: What Makes the World Cup Hit Different? Ask the Music Industry
news.lede: Music and football have always shared a connection — and as the World Cup final approaches, FIFA is highlighting the link like never before. The World Cup is in France. But a new era of globalization and international trade has created a rising demand for Latin music, and the world’s biggest soccer tournament needs anthem. Enter Puerto Rican singer Ricky Martin.

news.title: World Cup 2026: France's Michael Olise breaks Pelé's record for assists in a single tournament
news.lede: Kylian Mbappé wasn't the only France player to set a World Cup record in its 6-4 third-place game loss to England. As Mbappé scored his 22nd World Cup goal to break the all-time record set by Argentina's Lionel Messi earlier in the tournament, Olise provided the assist. That was Olise's seventh assist of the 2026 World Cup and broke a record held by Brazilian legend Pelé for over 50 years. Pelé had six assists in six games as Brazil won the 1970 World Cup.

news.title: Mbappé becomes World Cup history’s top scorer, watch 📹
news.lede: France lost to England 6-4 this Saturday (18) and finished only in fourth place at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The result marked Les Bleus' worst campaign with Mbappé in the squad and also the farewell of coach Didier Deschamps. The current world runners-up were also heavily MOCKED after conceding four goals in the first half! But after the break, when coach Didier Deschamps brought on the "cavalry," France finally decided to play.

news.title: World Cup 2026: Rate the players in France v England
news.lede: The World Cup third-place play-off between England and France - which you can follow here - has been quite a spectacle. You can rate both sets of players out of 10 below and come back 30 minutes after full-time to see the final ratings. After the opportunity to rate players has closed, the score displayed represents the average from all the submissions by BBC Sport users. World Cup fixtures and group standings.
